channel.title: Workshop | Ian Buller, Ph.D., M.A.
channel.copyright: © Ian Buller, 2026 · Opinions expressed are my own

item.title: NIH Research Festival 2025
item.description:
I co-instructed a workshop at the NIH Research Festival 2025 entitled “Geospatial for Everyone: Enhancing Your “Non-spatial” Research with Geospatial Data” with Dr. Nathaniel MacNell.

In our workshop, we presented an interactive open-source workflow of geographically linking publicly available data to simulated locations and demonstrated how these data can be used for clinical study design and analysis. We used the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI) & National Cancer Institute (NCI) Neighborhood Deprivation Index (NDI) throughout our examples, which is also calculated by my R package ndi (click here for more information). We were one of twenty-six vendor workshops held at the NIH Research Festival chosen out of hundreds of applicants.

item.title: Society for Epidemiologic Research 2024
item.description:
I co-instructed a workshop at the Society for Epidemiologic Research Annual Meeting 2024 entitled “Git’n up to speed on versioning control.”

The workshop was an introduction to the Git language and other Git-enabled technology for epidemiologists to version control their software code. I developed the course with other DLHers, including Dr. Ghassan Hamra, Dr. Nat MacNell, and Audrey Brown.

I was also a co-author on a poster abstract entitled “Revisiting the modifiable areal unit problem in the era of exposome-wide association studies: Assessing the performance of the CDC/ATSDR Social Vulnerability Index at privacy-protecting spatial scales” led by Jonathan Lewis.

item.title: Lightning Talk at NIEHS Workshop
item.description: I presented a lightning talk entitled “Geographic Variation in the Oral Microbiome of NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study Participants” at a National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences workshop entitled Impact of Environmental Exposures on the Microbiome and Human Health held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic.
